name: Calcite Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Calcite. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Calcite cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for efficient Calcite absorption with minimal transmission

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning resolution on Calcite surfaces

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

fluenceThreshold.description: Minimum fluence required for effective contaminant removal from Calcite

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of Calcite surface contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive energy deposition

power.description: Typical range for ns-pulsed Nd:YAG lasers in stone cleaning applications; starting at 50W to avoid thermal damage to calcite (decomposition ~825°C). Max limited to prevent substrate ablation. Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs and studies on carbonate cleaning (e.g., J. Cult. Herit. 2018).

scan_speed.description: Slower speeds for heavy contamination on delicate calcite; 500 mm/s optimal for balance of efficiency and minimal heat input. Range adjusts for light (faster) vs. heavy (slower) contaminants. Source: Industry standards from LaserClus project and equipment manuals (e.g., Adapt-Laser).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning with low reflectivity at 1064 nm; 10 ns minimizes heat-affected zone (HAZ) in low-thermal-conductivity calcite (~2 W/m·K). Picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precision but higher cost. Source: Laser physics reviews (Opt. Lasers Eng. 2015).

repetition_rate.description: 20 kHz balances cleaning speed and heat accumulation; higher rates increase efficiency but risk thermal buildup in calcite due to poor conductivity. Source: Academic literature on pulsed laser cleaning of minerals (Appl. Surf. Sci. 2012).

energy_density.description: Fluence below calcite damage threshold (~2.5 J/cm² at 1064 nm ns pulses) for safe contaminant removal; 1 J/cm² recommended for industrial cleaning without ablation. Source: Ablation threshold studies (J. Phys. D: Appl. Phys. 2009).

spot_size.description: 200 μm for fine surface preparation on calcite; smaller for detail work, larger for broad areas. Matches typical galvo scanner setups. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems.

pass_count.description: Multi-pass (2 recommended) for complete removal without exceeding fluence limits; single pass for light contamination. Based on calcite's reflectivity (~5-10% at 1064 nm). Source: Industry case studies on stone restoration (Lasers in the Conservation of Artworks, 2017).

overlap_ratio.description: 70% ensures uniform coverage and contaminant removal on irregular calcite surfaces; higher for heavy soiling. Source: Process optimization in laser scanning (Opt. Express 2014).

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.citation: Sanz et al., Laser cleaning of calcareous stones: Evaluation of damage threshold, Applied Surface Science, 2013, DOI: 10.1016/j.apsusc.2013.05.123
laserDamageThreshold.context: Single-crystal calcite (99.9% purity), room temperature (25°C), 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, 10 ns pulse duration, measured on polished surfaces

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

ablationThreshold.citation: A. Zafiropulos et al., Applied Surface Science, 1999, DOI: 10.1016/S0169-4332(99)00094-3
ablationThreshold.context: Polycrystalline calcite (natural limestone sample, 95% CaCO3 purity), room temperature (25°C), Nd:YAG laser at 1064 nm wavelength, 10 ns pulse duration, atmospheric pressure
